annotations.label: Real/complex Clifford algebras as matrix rings

anchor.section: Examples: real and complex Clifford algebras
anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to A or A ⊕ A , where A is a full matrix ring

annotations.note: The classification of real/complex Clifford algebras as matrix rings is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real nondegenerate form is standard diagonal

anchor.snippet: Every nondegenerate quadratic form on a finite-dimensional real vector space is equivalent to the standard diagonal form

annotations.note: Sylvester's law: a nondegenerate real form is equivalent to a weighted sum of squares with ±1 weights.

anchors.snippet: Every nondegenerate quadratic form on a finite-dimensional real vector space is equivalent to the standard diagonal form

annotations.label: Signature of a quadratic form

anchor.snippet: The pair of integers ( p , q ) is called the signature of the quadratic form

annotations.note: sigPos and sigNeg give the positive/negative parts (p,q) of the signature of a quadratic form.

anchors.snippet: The pair of integers ( p , q ) is called the signature of the quadratic form

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated ℝ^{p,q} space with a standard ±1 orthonormal basis definition.

annotations.label: Cl_{0,0}(R) ≅ R

anchor.snippet: Cl 0,0 ( R ) is naturally isomorphic to R since there are no nonzero vectors

annotations.note: CliffordAlgebraRing.equiv shows the Clifford algebra of a zero-dimensional space is isomorphic to R.

annotations.label: Cl_{0,1}(R) ≅ C

anchor.snippet: Cl 0,1 ( R ) is a two-dimensional algebra generated by e 1 that squares to −1

annotations.note: CliffordAlgebraComplex.equiv proves the 1-dim real Clifford algebra with e²=-1 is isomorphic to ℂ.

annotations.label: Cl_{1,0}(R) ≅ split-complex numbers

anchor.snippet: Cl 1,0 ( R ) is a two-dimensional algebra generated by e 1 that squares to 1

annotations.note: No Clifford-algebra isomorphism to the split-complex numbers is formalized.

annotations.note: CliffordAlgebraQuaternion.equiv with c₁=c₂=-1 gives the quaternions ℍ as a special case.

annotations.note: CliffordAlgebraQuaternion.equiv with c₁=c₂=1 yields the split-quaternion algebra ℍ[ℝ,1,0,1].

annotations.label: Cl_{0,3}(R) ≅ H ⊕ H

anchor.snippet: Cl 0,3 ( R ) is an 8-dimensional algebra isomorphic to the direct sum

annotations.note: The isomorphism Cl_{0,3}(ℝ) ≅ ℍ ⊕ ℍ is not formalized.

annotations.note: The Pauli algebra Cl_{3,0}(ℝ) and its biquaternion identification are not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex nondegenerate form is standard diagonal

annotations.note: Over an algebraically closed field (incl. ℂ) a nondegenerate form is equivalent to the all-ones sum of squares.
